message:
feat(abi): add pre-TLS bump allocator, refactor dirent, fix posix_spawn PATH lookup

malloc_abi — pre-TLS bootstrap bump allocator:
- Add 4 MiB static bump allocator to break infinite recursion during early
  process startup: malloc → native_libc_malloc_sym → __libc_malloc → malloc
- Each native_libc_{malloc,calloc,realloc,free} now uses atomic reentry guards;
  on reentry, allocations are served from the bump heap instead of recursing
- bump_alloc uses CAS on BUMP_POS for thread-safe 16-byte-aligned allocation
- native_libc_free skips bump-allocated pointers (free is a no-op for them)

dirent_abi — readdir refactoring:
- Extract write_dirent() helper that zero-initializes the dirent struct and
  copies d_ino, d_off, d_reclen, d_type, and NUL-terminated d_name in one place
- Both readdir paths (cached buffer and fresh getdents64) now call write_dirent
  instead of duplicating field-by-field copy logic
- fdopendir: replace zero-length getdents64 probe with fstat() + S_IFDIR check
  for more reliable directory validation

process_abi — posix_spawn PATH lookup:
- Replace std::env::var("PATH") with direct environ pointer traversal to avoid
  allocating during early process startup when the Rust allocator may not be
  ready, and to correctly handle binary PATH values

startup_abi:
- Add reentry guard for __cxa_thread_atexit_impl to prevent infinite recursion
  during first TLS access when Rust runtime registers its own TLS destructors

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
